title: Activated charcoal face wash
subtitle: A soap-base face wash with activated charcoal, aloe vera gel, and tea tree essential oil
excerpt: This face wash is built on a liquid castile soap base rather than a true lotion or emulsion. Activated charcoal adds adsorptive cleansing properties. Because this is diluted soap with additives, it has a short shelf life of 3 months once opened and separates between uses.
sourceNotes: Soap-base facial cleanser formulation based on natural cosmetics formulation guides. Activated charcoal adsorption properties from dermatology literature.

recipe.batchNotes: Keep batches small; 3-month shelf life makes large batches wasteful.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months from date of making. Shake before each use as the charcoal settles. Discard if the wash develops an off smell.

recipeTools.notes: For mixing in charcoal without lumps

glossaryTerms.definition: Charcoal that has been processed to create a very large surface area of pores. It adsorbs (binds to its surface) oils, dirt, and certain toxins. In a face wash it acts as a drawing cleanser for congested or oily skin.
The adhesion of molecules to a surface, as distinct from absorption (taking into a substance). Activated charcoal adsorbs impurities onto its porous surface rather than absorbing them into itself.
A pre-made liquid soap made from plant oils (usually olive oil) and potassium hydroxide. Used as a ready-made base for face washes and cleansers, avoiding the need to handle lye.
The gel from the inner leaf of the aloe vera plant, used in cosmetics for its soothing, hydrating properties. In a soap-base face wash it moderates the cleansing action of the castile soap.

content.text: of excess sebum and surface impurities.

moderates the wash's stripping effect. Because charcoal is denser than the liquid and will settle, the bottle must be shaken before each use. Shelf life is 3 months. Keep batches to 100 ml to avoid waste.

Weigh the castile soap, aloe vera gel, and glycerine into a small bowl and stir to combine. In a separate small cup, mix the activated charcoal powder with a teaspoon of the castile soap mixture to make a lump-free paste. Add this paste back to the main mixture and stir until the charcoal is evenly distributed. Add the tea tree essential oil and stir again. Pour into the pump bottle using a funnel.

Store at room temperature. Shake before each use; charcoal settles to the bottom within minutes. Shelf life is 3 months from date of making. Discard if the wash develops an off or sour smell. Use on damp skin, lather, and rinse thoroughly.
